items.headline: State law SB 79 presentation: housing near transit
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission scheduled a presentation on California's Abundant and Affordable Homes Near Transit Act (SB 79, 2025). The law affects how cities can zone and approve housing development near transit corridors, which may influence Sunnyvale's future development standards.

items.headline: 20-unit apartment building permitted on Page Street
items.summary: A new 20-unit multi-family complex is cleared to build at 426 Page St for $3.8 million. This is the only new apartment building in this permit batch, adding rental housing in a neighborhood where most recent development has been single ADUs.

items.headline: Santa Clara Tourism Improvement District renewed, contract with Discover Santa Clara
items.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on renewing the Tourism Improvement District and approved an agreement with Silicon Valley/Santa Clara DMO, Inc. (operating as Discover Santa Clara) to administer the district. The district generates revenue from a small assessment on hotel stays and visitor accommodations to fund tourism marketing and promotion.

items.headline: Vesting Tentative Parcel Map filed to merge four existing parcels
items.summary: A vesting tentative parcel map application was under City Council review to consolidate four separate parcels into fewer parcels. The specific location and intended use were not detailed in the agenda provided.

items.headline: Measure E site portion to become dedicated parkland
items.summary: The City Council is considering adoption of a park dedication ordinance that would designate part of the Measure E site as parkland. This expands public open space in Palo Alto.

items.headline: Baylands Golf Links driving range expansion financial review
items.summary: The council will review a financial feasibility study update for expanding the driving range at Baylands Golf Links on Tuesday, June 23. The study assesses whether the project is economically viable.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study Housing Element update
items.summary: The Planning Commission is scheduled to hold a study session on updating Cupertino's Housing Element. The agenda does not provide details on what changes are being considered or what the update would require.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study artificial turf policy
items.summary: The Planning Commission held a study session on June 22 to examine artificial turf regulations. This will likely lead to policy or design review changes affecting landscaping requirements for new development.
